steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The passage describes how the previous observation was "raising questions about thermoregulation in fungi." This shows a cause-and-effect sequence: the observation leads to further action.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
The blank fills "in a study ______ a previous observation." The observation (mushrooms appeared colder than surroundings) triggered the study. Test each choice:

A) excluded by = shut out or barred by. "A study excluded by a previous observation" means the observation blocked the study—opposite of what happened. Fails.

B) acknowledged in = recognized or noted in. "A study acknowledged in a previous observation" implies the study was merely mentioned in the observation, not that it followed from it. Fails.

C) released in = published or issued in. "A study released in a previous observation" is nonsensical—studies are not released inside observations. Fails.

D) prompted by = motivated or triggered by. "A study prompted by a previous observation" correctly shows the observation caused Moreno to conduct the study. Correct.
